message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Japan’s Prime Minister Abe declared an emergency to control the COVID-19 outbreak on April 7, 2020. He asked almost half of the population of Japan to reduce their personal contacts by 70–80%.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Object</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    This study estimates the effectiveness of that emergency declaration. Method: We applied a simple susceptible–infected–recovery model to data of patients with symptoms in Tokyo, Japan for January 14 – April 21 as of April 22. We estimate the reproduction number in four periods: R
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    before voluntary event cancellation and school closure (VECSC) which was introduced since February 27 to March 19, R
                    <jats:sub>v</jats:sub>
                    during the VECSC, R
                    <jats:sub>a</jats:sub>
                    after VECSC, and R
                    <jats:sub>e</jats:sub>
                    after the emergency declaration.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Results suggest that the value of R
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    was estimated as 1.267; its range was [1.214, 1.341]. However, R
                    <jats:sub>v</jats:sub>
                    was estimated as 2.360 [1.844, 2.623]. R
                    <jats:sub>a</jats:sub>
                    was estimated as 2.307 [2.035, 2.794] and R
                    <jats:sub>e</jats:sub>
                    was 0.462 [0.347, 0.514].
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Discussion and Concussion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>One must be reminded that these results reflect only those at two weeks after the emergency declaration. The reproduction number probably changed thereafter continuously.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Effect of emergency declaration for the COVID-19 outbreak in Tokyo, Japan in the first two weeks

reference.unstructured: Japan Times. Japan state of emergency to cover Tokyo, Osaka and five other prefectures https://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2020/04/06/national/japan-state-of-emergency-covid-19/#.XpPANv37RaQ [accessed on April 21,2020]
